sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Easing Fuels Strong Rebound

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Surge as Geopolitical Tensions Ease and AI Trade Rebounds
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed sharply higher today, rebounding after two days of losses, driven by positive developments in Middle East geopolitical conflicts and a renewed momentum in AI-related stocks. All three major indices recorded their best single-day performance since April.

items.headline: SanDisk Soars on AI Memory Demand
items.summary: SanDisk ($SNDK) jumped 14.50% today after multiple analysts raised price targets, citing strong demand driven by artificial intelligence.

items.headline: Coupang Rises as Data Breach Fine Below Expectations
items.summary: Coupang ($CPNG) gained 14.09% after Morgan Stanley indicated that a recent record data breach fine was below market expectations.

items.headline: KLA Climbs on Wafer Fab Equipment Spending
items.summary: KLA ($KLAC) rose 12.92% following increased analyst price targets, attributed to robust spending in wafer fabrication equipment.

items.headline: Oracle Falls on Mixed Q4 Results and Outlook
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) fell 8.53% after reporting mixed fourth-quarter results and guidance, leading to recalibrated analyst targets.

items.headline: Autodesk Declines Significantly
items.summary: Autodesk ($ADSK) saw a decline of 7.10% today.

items.headline: Adobe Slips Despite Strong Earnings, CFO Departure Announced
items.summary: Adobe Inc. ($ADBE) declined 4.91% despite posting upbeat second-quarter financial results and raising its full-year guidance, following the announcement of its CFO's departure.

items.headline: Sector Performance Mixed; Consumer Staples and Energy Lead
items.summary: Today saw a mixed performance across S&P 500 sectors. While seven out of eleven sectors ended in negative territory, the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR ($XLP) rose 1.7% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR ($XLE) gained 1.5%. The Information Technology ($XLK), Industrials ($XLI), and Materials ($XLB) sectors experienced declines of 2.3%, 3.4%, and 2.3% respectively. Despite tech sector declines, AI semiconductor giants showed strong individual performance, contributing significantly to the Nasdaq's rally.

items.headline: May PPI Exceeds Expectations; Initial Jobless Claims Rise
items.summary: The Producer Price Index (PPI) for May rose 1.1%, exceeding the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 0.6%, marking the highest monthly gain since November 2022. Core PPI, excluding volatile items, also surpassed expectations, rising 0.8%. Separately, initial claims for unemployment benefits increased by 4,000 to 229,000 for the week ended June 6, slightly above estimates.

items.headline: Middle East De-escalation Drives Risk-On Sentiment, Oil Prices Fall
items.summary: Positive developments regarding Middle East geopolitical conflicts, including President Trump's statements about a potential peace deal and cancellation of planned strikes on Iran, sparked a significant risk-on reversal in markets. This led to crude oil prices falling, with Brent crude dropping nearly 3% to settle just above $90/barrel.

items.headline: SpaceX IPO Finalized at $135 Per Share, Anticipated to Be Largest on Record
items.summary: Elon Musk's rocket and satellite maker, SpaceX, has officially finalized its initial public offering (IPO) price at $135 per share. The company is set to offer 555.56 million shares to raise $75 billion, valuing the company at $1.77 trillion, making it potentially the largest IPO on record. Investors are keenly awaiting its debut on Nasdaq.
